machine: Intel Core i7-11800H (Tiger Lake-H, 8c/16t), DDR4-3200 dual-channel, Windows 11, CPU-only (CUDA hidden via CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=-1)

model.name: Ternary-Bonsai-4B-TQ2_0
model.quant: TQ2_0 (2.06 bpw ternary linears) + Q6_K tied embed/lm_head

oracle: llama.cpp llama-server acd79d6, CPU-only (-ngl 0 -t 8), greedy (temperature 0, top_k 1), cache_prompt=false; /completion return_tokens
methodology: Both engines run the IDENTICAL TQ2_0 GGUF on CPU, greedy. Camelid bench-generate output_token_ids vs llama.cpp /completion tokens, raw completion (BOS added by both, no chat template), 24 generated tokens per prompt.

results.note: near-tie in a degenerate repeating continuation: camelid token 17 ('2') vs llama 19 ('4') after both correctly emit '2 + 2 = 4,'. Both numerically valid digit continuations.

parity.summary: 3/4 prompts greedy-match llama.cpp exactly for 24 tokens; 1 diverges at a near-tie (the low-bit precision floor, same class as the gemma4 Q4_0 lane). Prompt-token parity is implied by the from-token-0 match on the 3 exact prompts.
parity.verdict: greedy-token parity with documented near-tie divergence; NOT bit-exact (a quantized lane against a quantized oracle).

perf.note: Camelid is 0.53x llama.cpp on decode — the documented general forward-pass gap, not a ternary-kernel deficit (the TQ2_0 AVX2 dot is ~11% of decode; the Q6_K streamed head ~22%). This lane is a RAM-fitting, coherent, parity-checked FEATURE (a 4B ternary model in 3 GB), not a throughput win over llama.cpp.
perf.decode_history: scalar 2.23 -> AVX2+tiled 2.23 (TQ dot not the bottleneck) -> Q6_K streamed head 11.34 tok/s (5.1x; logits 415->18.6 ms/token)
